title: The Pawnee Eagleton Tip Off Classic

jokes.text: That's the sexiest thing you've ever said to me.
jokes.setup: Ben reads Leslie's text about slamming Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Leslie finds political attacks sexually arousing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Leslie's political obsession bleeding into her personal life

jokes.text: Look. Am I proud of it? Yes. Because Eagleton sucks. But is it the classiest move? Yes, because Eagleton sucks. Would I do it again? Yes, because Eagleton sucks.
jokes.setup: Leslie pretending to consider the morality of attacking Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Every rhetorical question gets the same answer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure showing Leslie's inability to be objective about Eagleton, with perfect escalating repetition

jokes.text: She thinks she's so great because she won the Miss Indiana Beauty Pageant. Last year. While she was in office. And pregnant.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why Ingrid thinks she's great
jokes.punchline: Each additional detail makes the accomplishment more impressive and Leslie's jealousy more petty
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating structure where each reveal makes Ingrid more accomplished and Leslie more petty

jokes.text: Oh, no, these are pesticide-free. I ate one of these for breakfast this morning, and I found a worm in it. So, I bet somebody feels pretty stupid right now.
jokes.setup: Ingrid dismisses Pawnee apples as pesticide-laden
jokes.punchline: Leslie's 'gotcha' moment actually proves Ingrid's point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection where Leslie's triumphant delivery makes her complete misunderstanding funnier

jokes.text: These are made of Swarovski crystal, and they're worth $700,000.
jokes.setup: Eagleton offering oranges as their wager
jokes.punchline: The oranges are worth nearly three-quarters of a million dollars
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist humor showing Eagleton's ridiculous wealth through a completely over-the-top detail

jokes.text: But when you tell 'em it's time to change their oil, they ask, 'Extra virgin or white truffle?'
jokes.setup: Leslie doing anti-Eagleton material about them being out of touch
jokes.punchline: Playing on oil/cooking oil confusion
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay joke but feels a bit constructed for Leslie's character

jokes.text: Unless, of course, you're an Eagletonian, and then you'll be too busy polishing your monocle at the caviar store.
jokes.setup: Leslie wrapping up her press conference
jokes.punchline: Combining two ridiculous rich person stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Functional rich person jokes but lacks the specificity that makes the better Eagleton material work

jokes.text: Knope out.
jokes.setup: Leslie ends her press conference
jokes.punchline: Pun on her name and 'Nope out'
jokes.explanation: Simple pun but perfectly fits Leslie's love of wordplay and self-promotion

jokes.text: Ebony was due to a clerical error, but it ended up being an interesting year of reading.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his family's magazine subscriptions
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery about accidentally reading a magazine for Black readers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - deadpan delivery of something unexpectedly progressive

jokes.text: I need my mitts on those mitts.
jokes.setup: April explaining her Williams-Sonoma addiction
jokes.punchline: Unexpected wordplay from the normally deadpan April
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's slightly out of character for April but works as a rare moment of enthusiasm

jokes.text: According to her Twitter feed, she got coffee five minutes ago. Wait, now she's Ustreaming her walk back. She should be here in three, two, one.
jokes.setup: April tracking Donna's return via social media
jokes.punchline: Perfect timing as Donna walks in on cue
jokes.explanation: Great social media oversharing joke with perfect timing payoff

jokes.text: Hey, Dr. Buttons-- I mean, my old calculator. It doesn't have a name.
jokes.setup: Ben catching his calculator that Chris threw
jokes.punchline: Almost revealing he named his calculator, then awkwardly covering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment showing his nerdiness and social awkwardness in one quick beat

jokes.text: Butch Count-sidy and the Sum-dance Kid, together again.
jokes.setup: Chris referencing their accounting partnership
jokes.punchline: Butch Cassidy pun with accounting terms
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Chris's enthusiastic personality perfectly

jokes.text: Auditing bros! Yeah, and auditing sistah. Let's do this.
jokes.setup: Chris says 'auditing bros'
jokes.punchline: Ben adds the inclusive 'sistah' correction
jokes.explanation: Shows their nerdy enthusiasm and Ben's consideration for Leslie

jokes.text: If you make your omelet out of Faberge eggs, you just might... Be an Eagletonian.
jokes.setup: Tom doing Jeff Foxworthy-style material about Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Faberge eggs instead of chicken eggs
jokes.explanation: Functional redneck comedy parody but not particularly inspired

jokes.text: I would get one in Pawnee, but fun fact-- our only baby store is attached to a chemical refinery.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why he's going to Bloomington for baby supplies
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning the horrifying fact about Pawnee's baby store
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor about Pawnee's dysfunction delivered with Andy's innocent tone

jokes.text: Damn it. You look beautiful.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to take an unflattering photo of Ingrid
jokes.punchline: His frustration that even candid shots look good
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Ben's inability to be truly mean

jokes.text: We don't really like to talk about money. We find a little gauche.
jokes.setup: Being asked about Eagleton's budget deficit
jokes.punchline: Finding money talk gauche while in bankruptcy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Eagleton obliviousness - being precious about money while broke

jokes.text: This is a budget meeting.
jokes.setup: Ingrid saying they don't like talking about money
jokes.punchline: Ben's exasperated statement of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Good straight man reaction to Ingrid's absurdity

jokes.text: Are you tipping me? Is that 100...Euros?
jokes.setup: Ingrid hands Chris money for helping
jokes.punchline: It's not just a tip, but foreign currency
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Eagleton's ridiculous wealth and disconnection from reality

jokes.text: Grid, grid, grid, grid, grid, grid, grid-- / No grid, no grid, no grid, no grid.
jokes.setup: Tom and April chanting about being on/off the grid
jokes.punchline: Turning into competing chants like a political rally
jokes.explanation: Fun visual moment but relies more on energy than wit

jokes.text: Yeah, an empty Sprite can, an old man's fingernail in my pocket, and a picture of Patrick Ewing on a roller coaster. I'm all set.
jokes.setup: Andy asking if April has everything for orientation
jokes.punchline: April's completely random and useless list of items
jokes.explanation: Perfect April absurdism - completely random items delivered with total confidence

jokes.text: I'm April Ludgate Kevorkian.
jokes.setup: April introducing herself to the vet school guide
jokes.punchline: Adding 'Kevorkian' (Dr. Death) to her name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that's perfectly in character for April's morbid sense of humor

jokes.text: What if I'm a murderer?
jokes.setup: Andy asking if he should leave April with the nice guide
jokes.punchline: April casually suggesting she might murder the guide
jokes.explanation: Classic April dark humor delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: Their debt-to-equity ratio is ridonkulous.
jokes.setup: Ben analyzing Eagleton's financial situation
jokes.punchline: Using 'ridonkulous' - a silly word for serious financial analysis
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Ben using outdated slang in professional context

jokes.text: What a hilarious word. It reminds me of a ridiculous donkey.
jokes.setup: Ben saying 'ridonkulous'
jokes.punchline: Chris breaking down the word into 'ridiculous donkey'
jokes.explanation: Fits Chris's enthusiastic word-nerd personality but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Now that is the sexiest thing you've ever said to me.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining Eagleton's financial disaster
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier 'sexiest thing' line
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Leslie's consistent priorities - politics over romance

jokes.text: Do you like apples? / How about them apples?
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to recreate Good Will Hunting scene
jokes.punchline: Ben's reluctance and knowledge of the reference ruins the moment
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-humor about forced movie references in real life

jokes.text: Passing the buck-- the last refuge of the cowardly and black-hearted.
jokes.setup: Mailman saying he doesn't control who gets mail
jokes.punchline: Ron's extremely dramatic response to bureaucratic deflection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron overreaction to minor bureaucracy - flowery language for simple complaint

jokes.text: These are the first bibs I've ever seen without Dale Earnhardt Jr.'s face on them.
jokes.setup: Andy looking at baby items in the fancy store
jokes.punchline: Revealing that NASCAR-branded baby bibs are normal in Pawnee
jokes.explanation: Great cultural detail that perfectly captures Pawnee's blue-collar character

jokes.text: We came from our bankruptcy brunch. And Michael Buble played, but he ran a little long.
jokes.setup: Ingrid apologizing for being late to the budget meeting
jokes.punchline: Having a fancy brunch during bankruptcy with expensive entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Eagleton obliviousness - spending money they don't have on ridiculous luxury

jokes.text: Was a bankruptcy brunch the best idea? / Sure, let's not have brunch... like animals.
jokes.setup: Ben questioning the wisdom of expensive brunch during bankruptcy
jokes.punchline: Ingrid treating basic frugality as barbarism
jokes.explanation: Perfect character clash - Ben's practical concern vs. Ingrid's entitlement

jokes.text: You filled the public pools with bottled water? / Total body hydration.
jokes.setup: Chris discovering an outrageous budget expenditure
jokes.punchline: Ingrid's matter-of-fact justification for wasteful spending
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail showing Eagleton's ridiculous excess with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: We purchased HBO for the whole town. / It's not TV.
jokes.setup: Being questioned about buying HBO with public money
jokes.punchline: Using HBO's own slogan as justification
jokes.explanation: Clever use of HBO's marketing slogan to justify wasteful government spending

jokes.text: 51 eggs in 20 minutes. Couldn't cook 'em fast enough. The last ten were still in the shell.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his egg-eating record at JJ's Diner
jokes.punchline: Escalating to eating raw eggs in shells
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - absurd masculine excess told with complete seriousness

jokes.text: That's too much information.
jokes.setup: Ron giving detailed instructions about obscuring his identity in the photo
jokes.punchline: Diner owner's deadpan response to Ron's paranoia
jokes.explanation: Good straight man reaction to Ron's excessive privacy concerns

jokes.text: Hello again. As you remember, I'm April, and this is Ann, my 65-year-old grandmother. / Mee-maw, God, no one uses the word hip anymore.
jokes.setup: April making Ann pretend to be her elderly grandmother
jokes.punchline: April calling her 'Mee-maw' and criticizing her word choice
jokes.explanation: Great cringe humor with April forcing Ann into uncomfortable roleplay

jokes.text: Sometimes I wish you went down on the Titanic with all your sorority sisters.
jokes.setup: April playing the role of disrespectful granddaughter
jokes.punchline: Wishing death on her fake grandmother in a historically specific way
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific insult that's perfectly in character for April's twisted humor

jokes.text: 'You blew it. Super hard. Complete buffoonery. It's hilarious.' And 'You deserve it.'
jokes.setup: Leslie reads from her prepared presentation
jokes.punchline: Increasingly harsh and petty slides attacking Eagleton
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's prepared pettiness escalating from criticism to cruelty

jokes.text: Whatever, blondie. Your butler made your bed, now you gotta lie in it.
jokes.setup: Leslie responding to Ingrid's fancy saying
jokes.punchline: Mangling the idiom by literally referencing butlers
jokes.explanation: Clever way to show Leslie's determination to be crude while staying somewhat witty

jokes.text: You don't kick a dressage horse after a failed pas de deux.
jokes.setup: Ingrid quotes an Eagleton saying
jokes.punchline: Extremely pretentious saying combining equestrian and ballet terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Eagleton pretension - combining two elite activities in one nonsensical saying

jokes.text: Oh, I've been taking pictures of you all day for my new Facebook album: 'Ronswan: Gettin' off the grid.'
jokes.setup: Ron asking what Tom is talking about regarding Jean-Ralphio
jokes.punchline: Tom documenting Ron's privacy quest on social media
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic humor - documenting someone's attempt at privacy destroys the very thing they're seeking

jokes.text: Oh, my God. They have Michael Buble on retainer.
jokes.setup: Leslie discovers more about Eagleton's spending
jokes.punchline: They keep a major recording artist on permanent payroll
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Eagleton's wasteful spending to celebrity level

jokes.text: You just vined your first selfie, Ron. And I'm vining you vine your selfie.
jokes.setup: Ron accidentally using social media apps
jokes.punchline: Tom creating meta-documentation of Ron's social media mishap
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about social media documentation spiraling out of control

jokes.text: The world is a nightmare.
jokes.setup: Ron realizing he's accidentally joined social media
jokes.punchline: His dramatic assessment of modern technology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response - treating minor inconvenience as existential crisis

jokes.text: Youngsters? Am I 65 years old?
jokes.setup: Ann using the word 'youngsters' to convince April
jokes.punchline: Accidentally referencing her fake age from earlier
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the grandmother bit with Ann inadvertently staying in character

jokes.text: Why do people keep assuming I'm ready to catch things?
jokes.setup: Ben dropping another thrown object
jokes.punchline: His exasperation at people's expectations of his coordination
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's lack of athletic ability and social awkwardness

jokes.text: 'Eagleton: No money, mo' problems.' Fantastic, right?
jokes.setup: Tom shows his t-shirt design
jokes.punchline: Biggie Smalls reference applied to municipal bankruptcy
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference twist for municipal politics merchandise

jokes.text: I have a PhD from the Sorbonne. For what? Wearing Chanel suits? There was a fashion component, yes.
jokes.setup: Ingrid defends her intelligence with PhD credential
jokes.punchline: Leslie's accusation turns out to be partially accurate
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that Leslie's dismissive assumption was actually somewhat accurate

jokes.text: What, Ann? I just stole your phone and texted every guy in it that the baby was theirs. It's not that big of a deal.
jokes.setup: Ann asking what happened in the car
jokes.punchline: April casually mentioning doing something terrible
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - doing something objectively horrible while treating it as minor

jokes.text: And I've met Guy Fieri, Ann. So gross!
jokes.setup: April saying Ann is the worst person she's ever met
jokes.punchline: Adding that she's met Guy Fieri for comparison
jokes.explanation: Good escalation using a specific pop culture reference that fits April's taste

jokes.text: There's no way he is in high school. And I'm better at French horn too, Eric.
jokes.setup: Leslie commenting on the opposing team's player
jokes.punchline: Randomly addressing someone named Eric about French horn
jokes.explanation: Random Leslie competitive outburst that shows her need to win at everything

jokes.text: The address is wherever it is parked.
jokes.setup: Dealer asking for an address for RV insurance
jokes.punchline: Ron's logical but unhelpful response
jokes.explanation: Good Ron logic - technically correct but missing the point of bureaucracy

jokes.text: I love you, but your solution to every problem is to live inside a mountain.
jokes.setup: Ron suggesting they move to underground tunnels
jokes.punchline: Diane pointing out Ron's consistent mountain-living suggestions
jokes.explanation: Perfect character observation that rings true to Ron's consistent worldview

jokes.text: I hate their stupid beautiful faces and their stupid shiny hair.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to express hatred for Eagletonians
jokes.punchline: Accidentally complimenting them while trying to insult them
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie can't even insult Eagleton properly

jokes.text: No one bailed me out when I got arrested for arson.
jokes.setup: Citizen asking why they should bail out Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning their arson arrest
jokes.explanation: Good background character moment revealing Pawnee's criminal element

jokes.text: What's next-- merging a man with his dog?
jokes.setup: Citizen objecting to merging towns
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous slippery slope argument
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist logic from a confused citizen

jokes.text: They're a little-- oh, what's the word? Have-no-money.
jokes.setup: Eagleton resident describing Pawnee residents
jokes.punchline: Looking for a polite way to say 'poor'
jokes.explanation: Good Eagleton snobbery - trying to be polite while being condescending

jokes.text: They're a town of angels-- greasy, denim-clad angels, but angels nonetheless.
jokes.setup: Ingrid defending the merger decision
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment calling Pawnee residents greasy and denim-clad
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ingrid moment - trying to be gracious while being classist

jokes.text: It looks like the original phone from the movie Wall Street. / I think it's just the original phone, period. / Ew, gross, it has 'buttons.'
jokes.setup: Family discussing Ron's emergency cell phone
jokes.punchline: Escalating from movie reference to ancient technology to daughter's disgust
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor about technology differences

jokes.text: Yeah, I can't be seen with you.
jokes.setup: Ron showing off his cell phone belt clip
jokes.punchline: Diane's immediate rejection of being associated with him
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Ron's fashion disaster - belt clips are universally mocked

jokes.text: A cell phone belt clip? No, Ron. Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Ron shows off his phone's clip feature
jokes.punchline: Diane's horror at the ultimate uncool accessory
jokes.explanation: Belt clips are universally mocked fashion choice - Diane's embarrassment is relatable

jokes.text: No. Dude, I'm, like, three months pregnant. I-I know. I'm just so excited. The thing I'm having is a gut feeling.
jokes.setup: Andy asking if Ann is having a contraction
jokes.punchline: Ann clarifying she means a 'gut feeling' not labor pains
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection playing on pregnancy terminology and April's advice about gut feelings

jokes.text: All of Pawnee's apples have been recalled-- g. Coli. / You mean e. Coli? / I wish. Way easy to treat.
jokes.setup: Leslie revealing the apples have been recalled for contamination
jokes.punchline: The contamination is worse than E. coli
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation revealing Pawnee's apples are even worse than we thought, with great misdirection
